title: Bridge Essay: A review of joint attention and social-cognitive brain systems in typical develo

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.

**Areview of joint attention and social-cognitive brain systems in typical development and autism spectrum disorder.** — Eur J Neurosci. PMID: 28922520. DOI: 10.1111/ejn.13720. This article provides areview of the increasingly detailed imaging literature on the neurodevelopment of joint attention. Many findings from this literature support and inform the hypothesis that the neurodevelopment of joint attention contributes to the functional development of neural systems for human social cognition. Joint attention begins to develop by 5 months of age and is tantamount to the ability to adopt acommon perspective with another person. It involves awhole-brain system with...

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 17 (Social Incarnation). Evaluation: Marginal (score=1.8)
